kept.text:
Advanced Micro Devices は予想を上回る見通し

Advanced Micro Devices ( $AMD ) は、2026年5月5日火曜日の午後4時15分（東部標準時）頃に決算発表を行うことが確定しました。
市場予想のコンセンサスは、売上高98億4000万ドルに対して1株当たり利益1.29ドルとなっており、
これは前年比32.3%の増収を意味します。また、アーニング・ウィスパー値は1株当たり1.36ドルです。
同社のガイダンスでは、売上高95億ドルから101億ドルに対し、1株当たり利益0.93ドルから1.10ドルとされていました。
投資家は同社の決算発表に向けて強気であり、79.6%が予想を上回ると期待しています。
前回の決算発表以降、空売り残高は6.6%減少しており、一方で株価は決算後の始値から67.7%上昇し、
200日移動平均線の210.37ドルを71.4%上回る水準まで上昇しています。
前回の決算発表以来、全体的な収益予想は上方修正されています。
オプション・トレーダーは決算による9.6%の価格変動を織り込んでいますが、最近の四半期における株価の平均変動率は7.5%です。
2026年5月1日金曜日には、2026年6月18日木曜日に期限を迎える320.00ドルのコール・オプションが
32,992契約という注目すべき規模で購入されました。

kept.text:
In this clip, $AMD CEO Lisa Su said the world will likely need 100x more compute over the next four to five years as AI workloads expand across industries.

She introduced the idea of the “yadaflop” to describe the next scale of computing, highlighting how future models will require orders of magnitude more performance than today’s systems.

The message is clear that we are still early in the infrastructure buildout phase, with demand for accelerators and supporting silicon continuing to expand as AI adoption moves toward billions of users globally.

kept.text:
BREAKING $AMD New Global Leader of Executive Talent Acquisition 🆕

Austin, Texas, United States, May 2026 – Daniel Clarkson has joined AMD as Global Leader of Executive Talent Acquisition, marking a significant step in his leadership journey in global talent strategy. Based in Austin, he will lead the development of a world-class executive hiring function, partnering closely with senior leadership to attract top-tier talent and support AMD’s next phase of innovation in high-performance computing and artificial intelligence.

Daniel Clarkson joins AMD with deep expertise in executive talent acquisition and global recruiting strategy. In his new role, he is focused on building and leading a best-in-class executive search organization aligned with the rapid evolution of the semiconductor industry, identifying visionary leaders who will shape the future of computing.

Prior to AMD, he served as Director Talent Acquisition at Microsoft, where he led talent acquisition for gaming and design functions across North America, partnering with senior leadership to drive innovative recruitment strategies and optimize hiring processes.

Earlier, Daniel spent nearly five years at LinkedIn, where he held multiple leadership roles including Head of Global Executive Talent Acquisition and Director of Leadership & Executive Talent Acquisition. During this period, he led global executive hiring strategies across engineering, product, and corporate functions, playing a critical role in building leadership pipelines for a rapidly growing organization.

Before LinkedIn, he spent over eight years at Google, where he held roles such as Global Staffing Manager and Staffing Lead – EMEA, leading global hiring strategies across business and technical functions and managing large, distributed recruitment teams across regions.

His earlier career includes leadership roles in recruitment and talent consulting across global markets, where he built strong expertise in executive search, engineering talent acquisition, and team leadership.

tweet.text:
Leading Semiconductor Stocks

$MXL (MaxLinear):
Cyclical comms + optical recovery play with upside torque as inventory correction bottoms

$MRVL (Marvell Technology):
AI datacenter networking + custom silicon + optical = core hyperscaler enabler

$AMD (Advanced Micro Devices):
Only scaled alternative to Nvidia across CPU + GPU + AI accelerators

$MPWR (Monolithic Power Systems):
AI power delivery king → direct leverage to 48V→1V rack architecture shift

$TXN (Texas Instruments):
Analog monopoly + internal fabs → unmatched margins + FCF as industrial cycle rebounds

$STM (STMicroelectronics):
EV + industrial secular growth with Europe-backed strategic positioning

$QUIK (QuickLogic):
Ultra-small edge AI/FPGA bet → asymmetric upside on low-power inference adoption

$NXPI (NXP Semiconductors):
Auto + industrial rebound + software-defined vehicle tailwinds

$QCOM (Qualcomm):
Leader in on-device AI → expanding from mobile into PC + automotive

$CRDO (Credo Technology):
High-speed connectivity (AECs/DSP) → critical bottleneck in scaling AI clusters

$ALAB (Astera Labs):
CXL + PCIe connectivity leader → enabling composable AI infrastructure

$INTC (Intel):
Foundry + packaging + AI turnaround optionality → massive upside if execution stabilizes

tweet.text:
Gençlerin Portföyü | Hafta Özeti 📊
Portföyümüz geçen haftanın ATH seviyesinden yaklaşık 1000$’lık düşüşle kapattı.
Haftanın açık ara 3 yıldızı şöyleydi:
•  $GOOGL %10,52
•  $AMD %7,74’lük yükselişle yeniden ATH yaptı
•  $NBIS %6,62 yükselişle onları takip etti
Haftanın hayal kırıklıkları ise $HOOD ve $META oldu. Bilançolar beğenilmedi ve hak etmedikleri satışlara maruz kaldılar. Hedefimiz bu hafta bu hisselere ekleme yapmak.
Portföyün yeni üyesi $RKLB ile yazdığımız covered call işlemi sayesinde prim toplanmış ve teminattaki hisseler cuma kapanışı ile serbest kalmıştı. Pazartesi açılışıyla birlikte agresif bir covered call daha yazarak prim toplamayı ve bu primleri düşen hisselerimize ekleme yapmak için kullanmayı planlıyoruz.
$RKLB’nin amacı diğer hisselerimizden farklı olarak; opsiyon ticaretiyle düzenli prim toplamak ve bu primleri tekrar portföye yönlendirmek.
Özetle: Portföyümüz 2 büyük düşüşü 3 güçlü yükselişle karşıladı ve haftayı çok az kayıpla kapattı.
Gelecek hafta özetiyle görüşmek üzere;
Sağlıklı, mutlu ve bol kazançlı bir hafta dilerim! 🙌✨

tweet.text:
Next week is huge for photonics & semiconductor Earnings.

Summary of key signals that could be good/bad for companies like $FN to $AAOI to $LITE.

TLDR:
- $AMD, $SMCI & $KLIC - ultimately set the tone for the entire AI infrastructure complex.
- $LITE, $COHR, $AAOI commentary on 1.6T mix + CPO timing is the most important data for exposure into H2 2026.

> Monday:

$FN: any new 1.6T/CPO wins + capacity plans. Also any confirmation that 1.6T transceivers for $NVDA Blackwell Ultra are in volume production. Customer concentration w/ Nvidia + Cisco is probs the main swing variable rn.

$PLTR (since it’s probs a huge position for most ppl): currently priced for a flawless beat+raise. The only thing that matters is whether US Commercial rev sustains >130% YoY pace + whether mgmt raises FY26 guide again. Anything less than a meaningful raise risks multiple compression even on a headline beat.

> Tuesday:

$LITE: bar is super high. Already guided Q3 to ~$830M (+85% YoY) + "multi-hundred-million-dollar" CPO order for H1 2027 delivery. Narrative needs to hold: (a) transceiver ramp (b) any clarity on optical scale-up (c) margin trajectory toward 30% guide. Operating profit is tiny, so any cost slip on capacity adds could be negative.

$AMD: data center rev beat+raise. Would need clear path to share gains in training/inference without margin give-back. So, opex/data center guide that doesn't spook algos. Also updates on $200B supply from OpenAI to prove AMD is eroding $NVDA mkt share.

$SMCI: room for a re-rate if margins inflect. Other signals incl. positive Blackwell $13B order book shipments, backlog conversion, hyperscaler mix, and any raise to $40B+ FY2026 rev target. Any softening in demand / more margin decompression highlights pricing power weakness vs. $DELL. The governance overhang from 2024 is still fresh in the memory, so need positive commentary on that.

> Wednesday:

$COHR: need a clean beat like $LITE. Info that 6-inch InP yields are exceeding legacy lines to support high margin H2 production ramp of 1.6T transceivers. Also confirmation of initial CPO rev from "exceptionally large purchase order" CEO said on Q2 call.

$KLIC: advanced-packaging pivot is the entire story - key is order momentum in advanced packaging/memory, margin stabilization & any FY2026 growth reaffirmation. The AI-packaging optionality (HBM, HBF) is what could re rate their multiple.

$IONQ: Feel like this is less about fundamentals since quantum is still so early, but will be important for all Quantum names like $QBTS, $RGTI since they all move in tandem it seems. Key signals are potentially (a) cash burn after recent capital raises (b) backlog colour underpinning $225M FY26 guide.

> Thursday:

$AAOI: key watchpoints are 800G/1.6T shipment traction, recent hyperscaler order backlog (e.g. $71M upsized 800G), margin trajectory, Texas capacity ramp. Mgmt say demand "outpaces capacity through mid-2027" so any execution slip would be punished. Ofc, new order announcements would be v. nice.

Just top level summary points that are most important imo for each company rn.
